segments.text: This is what the LORD says:
Go down to the palace of the king of Judah and proclaim this message there,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: saying, ‘Hear the word of the LORD, O king of Judah, who sits on the throne of David—you and your officials and your people who enter these gates.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is what the LORD says: Administer justice and righteousness. Rescue the victim of robbery from the hand of his oppressor. Do no wrong or violence to the foreigner, the fatherless, or the widow. Do not shed innocent blood in this place.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For if you will indeed carry out these commands, then kings who sit on David’s throne will enter through the gates of this palace riding on chariots and horses—they and their officials and their people.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if you do not obey these words, then I swear by Myself, declares the LORD, that this house will become a pile of rubble.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For this is what the LORD says concerning the house of the king of Judah:
You are like Gilead to Me, like the summit of Lebanon; but I will surely turn you into a desert, like cities that are uninhabited.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will appoint destroyers against you, each man with his weapons, and they will cut down the choicest of your cedars and throw them into the fire.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And many nations will pass by this city and ask one another, ‘Why has the LORD done such a thing to this great city?’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then people will reply, ‘Because they have forsaken the covenant of the LORD their God and have worshiped and served other gods.’
segments.speaker: Nations

segments.text:
Do not weep for the dead king; do not mourn his loss. Weep bitterly for the one who is exiled, for he will never return
to see his native land.
For this is what the LORD says concerning Shallum son of Josiah, king of Judah, who succeeded his father Josiah but has gone forth from this place:
He will never return,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: but he will die in the place to which he was exiled; he will never see this land again.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Woe to him who builds his palace by unrighteousness, and his upper rooms without justice, who makes his countrymen serve without pay, and fails to pay their wages,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
who says, ‘I will build myself a great palace, with spacious upper rooms.’So he cuts windows in it, panels it with cedar,
and paints it with vermilion.
segments.speaker: Unrighteous King

segments.text: Does it make you a king to excel in cedar? Did not your father have food and drink? He administered justice and righteousness, and so it went well with him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He took up the cause of the poor and needy, and so it went well with him. Is this not what it means to know Me?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
But your eyes and heart are set on nothingexcept your own dishonest gain, on shedding innocent blood, on practicing extortion and oppression.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore this is what the LORD says concerning Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah:
They will not mourn for him:‘Alas, my brother! Alas, my sister!’They will not mourn for him:‘Alas, my master! Alas, his splendor!’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: He will be buried like a donkey, dragged away and thrown outside the gates of Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Go up to Lebanon and cry out; raise your voice in Bashan; cry out from Abarim, for all your lovers have been crushed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I warned you when you were secure. You said, ‘I will not listen.’This has been your way from youth, that you have not obeyed My voice.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The wind will drive away all your shepherds, and your lovers will go into captivity. Then you will be ashamed and humiliatedbecause of all your wickedness.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: O inhabitant of Lebanon, nestled in the cedars, how you will groan when pangs of anguish come upon you, agony like a woman in labor.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: As surely as I live,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD,
even if you, Coniah son of Jehoiakim king of Judah, were a signet ring on My right hand, I would pull you off.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: In fact, I will hand you over to those you dread, who want to take your life—to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and to the Chaldeans.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will hurl you and the mother who gave you birth into another land, where neither of you were born—and there you both will die.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You will never return to the land for which you long.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Is this man Coniah a despised and shattered pot, a jar that no one wants? Why are he and his descendants hurled out and cast into a land they do not know?
O land, land, land, hear the word of the LORD!
This is what the LORD says:

Enroll this man as childless, a man who will not prosper in his lifetime. None of his descendants will prosperto sit on the throne of David
or to rule again in Judah.
segments.speaker: the Lord
